note: Adding compression (gzip/zlib) to Cache class (constructor params compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy, put/get serialization with DSPC magic header), plus set_compression method. Also extending configure_cache() in __init__.py with compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and passing them to Cache().

content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
spec: Add optional on-disk compression to the Cache class. Constructor: def __init__(self, enable_disk_cache, enable_memory_cache, disk_cache_dir, disk_size_limit_bytes=10485760, memory_max_entries=1000000, compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None). New params: compress: Optional[Literal['gzip','zlib']]=None - store as self.compress = compress. cull_limit: Optional[int]=None and eviction_policy: Optional[str]=None - pass through to FanoutCache(...) constructor call only when not None, e.g. build a kwargs dict and add 'cull_limit': cull_limit if cull_limit is not None, 'eviction_policy': eviction_policy if eviction_policy is not None (FanoutCache/diskcache defaults are cull_limit=10, eviction_policy='least-recently-stored' - do not override unless explicitly passed). Add method set_compression(self, codec: Optional[str]) -> None that sets self.compress = codec (validate codec in (None,'gzip','zlib') else raise ValueError). Serialization: On put(), when enable_disk_cache and self.compress is set, before writing to self.disk_cache[key] = value, instead do: payload = cloudpickle.dumps(value); if self.compress == 'gzip': payload = gzip.compress(payload); codec_byte = b'\x01'; elif self.compress == 'zlib': payload = zlib.compress(payload); codec_byte = b'\x02'; else codec_byte = b'\x00'; final_bytes = b'DSPC' + codec_byte + payload; self.disk_cache[key] = final_bytes (store as raw bytes, diskcache will pickle the bytes object itself which is fine since bytes round-trip exactly). If self.compress is None, keep existing behavior of storing value directly (do NOT wrap with header) so pre-existing non-compression behavior/tests are unaffected -- ONLY use the header+bytes path when self.compress is not None, OR when reading back always attempt to detect header. On get(), when reading from disk_cache: raw = self.disk_cache[key]; if isinstance(raw, (bytes, bytearray)) and raw[:4] == b'DSPC': codec_byte = raw[4:5]; payload = raw[5:]; if codec_byte == b'\x01': payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == b'\x02': payload = zlib.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == b'\x00': pass; response = cloudpickle.loads(payload); else: response = raw (legacy raw object, no header - use as-is, this guarantees backward compatibility with old cache entries and with compress=None entries). Import gzip, zlib, and Literal (from typing) at top of file. This must round-trip arbitrary picklable objects (including pydantic/dspy Prediction objects) identically for both gzip and zlib, and must not break existing behavior when compress=None (default).

New params: compress: Optional[Literal['gzip','zlib']]=None - store as self.compress = compress. cull_limit: Optional[int]=None and eviction_policy: Optional[str]=None - pass through to FanoutCache(...) constructor call only when not None, e.g. build a kwargs dict and add 'cull_limit': cull_limit if cull_limit is not None, 'eviction_policy': eviction_policy if eviction_policy is not None (FanoutCache/diskcache defaults are cull_limit=10, eviction_policy='least-recently-stored' - do not override unless explicitly passed). Serialization: On put(), when enable_disk_cache and self.compress is set, before writing to self.disk_cache[key] = value, instead do: payload = cloudpickle.dumps(value); if self.compress == 'gzip': payload = gzip.compress(payload); codec_byte = b'\x01'; elif self.compress == 'zlib': payload = zlib.compress(payload); codec_byte = b'\x02'; else codec_byte = b'\x00'; final_bytes = b'DSPC' + codec_byte + payload; self.disk_cache[key] = final_bytes (store as raw bytes, diskcache will pickle the bytes object itself which is fine since bytes round-trip exactly). If self.compress is None, keep existing behavior of storing value directly (do NOT wrap with header) so pre-existing non-compression behavior/tests are unaffected -- ONLY use the header+bytes path when self.compress is not None, OR when reading back always attempt to detect header. On get(), when reading from disk_cache: raw = self.disk_cache[key]; if isinstance(raw, (bytes, bytearray)) and raw[:4] == b'DSPC': codec_byte = raw[4:5]; payload = raw[5:]; if codec_byte == b'\x01': payload = gzip.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == b'\x02': payload = zlib.decompress(payload); elif codec_byte == b'\x00': pass; response = cloudpickle.loads(payload); else: response = raw (legacy raw object, no header - use as-is, this guarantees backward compatibility with old cache entries and with compress=None entries). This must round-trip arbitrary picklable objects (including pydantic/dspy Prediction objects) identically for both gzip and zlib, and must not break existing behavior when compress=None (default).

content:
type: DONE
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implemented both compression (compress param, magic-header DSPC+codec, Cache.set_compression, cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough to FanoutCache) and TTL (ttl param, TTLCache for memory, disk_cache.set(expire=ttl)/expire() on miss, set_ttl() helper) per your spec. Verified: round-trip gzip/zlib/none, legacy no-header entries still readable, TTL expiry works for both layers, existing test suite (tests/clients, tests/caching) passes except 3 pre-existing unrelated failures in test_lm.py (confirmed failing on main too). Patch written to patch.txt.
